sales-tax.customIntro: Missouri has one of the most complex sales tax structures in the US — a modest 4.225% state rate that balloons significantly once city, county, and special district taxes are layered on top, pushing combined rates in Kansas City and St. Louis above 10% in some zip codes.
sales-tax.formula: Price × 0.0822 = sales tax owed (Missouri average example)

taxFreeWeekends.name: Back-to-School Sales Tax Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.items: Clothing $100 or less per item, school supplies $50 or less per purchase, computer software $350 or less, personal computers and peripherals $1,500 or less

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Missouri?
faqs.answer: Missouri's state sales tax rate is 4.225%. However, cities, counties, and special taxing districts all add their own rates on top, making Missouri one of the states with the widest variation in combined rates. Kansas City and St. Louis can exceed 10%, while rural areas may be close to 6%.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax in Kansas City, Missouri?
faqs.answer: Kansas City has a combined sales tax rate of approximately 10.25%, made up of the 4.225% state rate plus Jackson County and city taxes. The exact rate can vary slightly by zip code within the city due to multiple overlapping special taxing districts.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have a sales tax holiday in 2026?
faqs.answer: Yes. Missouri holds an annual back-to-school sales tax holiday on the first Friday through Sunday of August. Qualifying items include clothing priced at $100 or less per item, school supplies at $50 or less per purchase, and personal computers at $1,500 or less.

faqs.question: What is the Missouri sales tax receipt coin?
faqs.answer: Missouri sales tax tokens were small aluminum or zinc coins issued from 1935 to 1961 to make change when purchases fell below a full cent of sales tax. They are now collectible items with no legal tender value, but they are frequently searched online by collectors and history enthusiasts.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Missouri?
faqs.answer: Yes. Missouri charges sales tax on used vehicle purchases at the combined local rate for the county where the vehicle is registered. The tax is paid at the time of title transfer at the county license office.

faqs.question: Is there sales tax on groceries in Missouri?
faqs.answer: Missouri taxes most groceries at a reduced state rate of 1.225% instead of the full 4.225% state rate. Local taxes still apply on top, so the effective rate on groceries varies by location but is lower than the standard combined rate.
